experiments.label: baseline
experiments.comment: Reference fast-dev baseline on the MLX path. Compare every other run against this before reading too much into noise.

experiments.label: rope_5k
experiments.comment: Lower RoPE base. Cheap positional change to see whether the run looks immediately worse or unstable.

experiments.label: rope_20k
experiments.comment: Higher RoPE base. Another very local positional change that is easy to reason about.

experiments.label: kv2
experiments.comment: Reduce KV heads from 4 to 2. Good first architecture knob because it is simple and directly tied to attention capacity/compression tradeoffs.

experiments.label: kv8
experiments.comment: Increase KV heads to full MHA-style 8. Useful as a contrast against the more compressed GQA baseline.

experiments.label: mlp3
experiments.comment: Increase MLP multiplier from 2 to 3. This is a common first ablation because it changes capacity in one obvious place.

experiments.label: qk_gain_1p0
experiments.comment: Lower QK gain init. Good for checking whether early optimization becomes calmer or weaker.

experiments.label: qk_gain_2p0
experiments.comment: Raise QK gain init. Useful opposite-side check to see whether attention sharpness helps or destabilizes the first 50 steps.

experiments.label: tied_embed_lr_0p08
experiments.comment: Slightly higher tied-embedding learning rate. Embedding/head learning rate is a plausible early sensitivity knob.

experiments.label: matrix_lr_0p03
experiments.comment: Slightly lower matrix learning rate. Good first optimizer ablation without changing optimizer families.
